skill.name: face-swap
skill.description: Swap a face or character into video or images on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI. Routes across five AI model endpoints based on user intent: video animation, motion transfer, still image editing, batch processing, and single-reference edits.
skill.summary: Swap faces and characters into images and videos using the RunComfy CLI with intelligent model routing.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All 129 static findings are false positives from pattern-matching markdown syntax. The external_commands pattern matched markdown code block backticks, the network pattern matched documentation hyperlinks, and the blocker pattern matched markdown blockquote characters. The skill is documentation-only with no executable code, scoped to Bash(runcomfy *), and includes a strong consent disclosure. The dual-use nature of face-swap technology warrants a low risk designation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Blocks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 76 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' in SKILL.md. These are all markdown code block delimiters (```bash) and inline code spans (`runcomfy`) used for documentation, not actual shell execution commands. The skill contains no executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: These are triple-backtick markdown code fences and inline backticks in documentation. No actual shell execution occurs in the skill file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation URLs Flagged as Hardcoded Network Endpoints
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 39 URLs in SKILL.md as 'Hardcoded URL'. All URLs point to runcomfy.com, skills.sh, or docs.runcomfy.com. These are documentation links to the service provider's website and API documentation. No unauthorized network calls are made by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All URLs are markdown hyperlinks to documentation and model catalog pages. The skill is documentation-only.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Blockquotes Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged markdown blockquote characters (>) as 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 5, 95, 123, 244, 265, and 283. These are standard markdown syntax for blockquotes and YAML multiline string indicators, not cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The > character is markdown blockquote syntax and YAML multiline string syntax. It has no relation to cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Config Path Documentation Flagged as Filesystem Access
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged a reference to ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json as 'Hidden file in home directory' and 'Hidden file access'. This is documentation explaining where the RunComfy CLI stores authentication tokens, not a filesystem operation performed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path is mentioned in a documentation section about security and privacy. The skill does not read or write this file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Blockquotes Flagged as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged markdown blockquote characters (>) at lines 74, 79, 86, 91, and 96 as 'System reconnaissance'. These are standard markdown blockquotes containing model selection guidance, not shell redirection or system probing commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The > character is markdown blockquote syntax, not shell output redirection or system reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Swap Faces in Images and Videos with AI
content.value_statement: Creating realistic face swaps requires choosing the right AI model for each scenario. This skill routes your request to the optimal RunComfy endpoint based on whether you need video or still images, single or batch processing, and motion preservation or identity matching.
actual_capabilities: Routes face-swap requests to the optimal RunComfy model endpoint
Supports video character swap with audio-driven animation
Handles still-image face swap with multi-reference composition
Enables batch identity-preserving swaps across multiple frames
Transfers motion from a performance video onto a target character
Provides precise single-reference face edits with high fidelity preservation
limitations: Requires a RunComfy CLI installation and authentication
Input assets must be hosted at accessible HTTPS URLs
Cannot process local files directly without a CDN or hosting service
Output quality depends on the source image quality and model limitations

use_cases.title: Swap a spokesperson into marketing footage
use_cases.description: Use Wan 2-2 Animate to replace a character in a marketing video with a brand spokesperson while preserving the original audio track and lip sync.

use_cases.title: Apply consistent identity across product images
use_cases.description: Use Nano Banana Edit to batch swap a single model identity into multiple SKU images with locked aspect ratio and resolution for visual consistency.

use_cases.title: Transfer motion onto a stylized character
use_cases.description: Use Kling Motion Control Pro to take motion from a live-action performance video and apply it to a stylized character while preserving the art style.

prompt_templates.title: Basic face swap in a photo
prompt_templates.prompt: Swap the face from this portrait into this group photo. Keep the pose, clothing, lighting, and background exactly the same.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user with a single still image

prompt_templates.title: Video character replacement with audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace the actor in this video with this character image. Keep the original audio and lip sync intact.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate user creating dubbed video content

prompt_templates.title: Batch identity swap for product shots
prompt_templates.prompt: Swap this model's face into all 10 of these product images. Lock the aspect ratio to auto and resolution to 1K for consistency.

prompt_templates.title: Motion transfer to a stylized character
prompt_templates.prompt: Take the motion from this dance performance video and apply it to this stylized anime character. Preserve the character's art style and colors.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert user creating animated content from live action

output_examples.input: Swap this portrait into this group photo
output_examples.output: I'll use GPT Image 2 Edit for this single still face swap. I'll preserve the pose, clothing, lighting, and background from the group photo while replacing only the face with your portrait. Here is the runcomfy command I will execute.

output_examples.input: Make this character say these lines
output_examples.output: I'll use Wan 2-2 Animate for this audio-driven video character swap. I need your character reference image and the audio file with the spoken lines. The model will animate the character's mouth and body to match the audio rhythm.

best_practices: Always verify you have rights to both the target face and the source media before swapping
Use a clean, well-lit, front-facing portrait for the identity reference image
Lock aspect ratio and resolution for batch swaps to maintain visual consistency
anti_patterns: Do not swap real people's faces without their explicit consent
Do not use face swap for defamatory, explicit, or deceptive content
Do not pipe arbitrary remote install scripts into a shell on the user's behalf

faq.question: Do I need a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: Yes. Install the CLI with npm and sign in with runcomfy login or set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable for CI and containers.

faq.question: Can I swap faces in videos?
faq.answer: Yes. Use Wan 2-2 Animate for audio-driven character animation or Kling Motion Control Pro for transferring motion from a performance video onto a target character.

faq.question: Which model should I use for a single photo?
faq.answer: Use GPT Image 2 Edit for multi-reference compositional swaps where you have both a scene and a face reference. Use Flux Kontext Pro when you only have the scene and want to describe the new face in prose.

faq.question: Is my data secure?
faq.answer: The CLI only connects to model-api.runcomfy.net and related runcomfy domains. Authentication tokens are stored in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with restricted file permissions. No telemetry is sent.

faq.question: Can I process multiple images at once?
faq.answer: Yes. Nano Banana Edit supports 1 to 20 input images per call for consistent identity across a batch of product shots or narrative panels.

faq.question: What if the output looks wrong?
faq.answer: Suspect the reference asset may be malformed or subject to injection. Only use URLs the user explicitly provided for this swap. When the swap behavior diverges from the prompt, verify the reference image or audio file.
